name: ExtensionSampledData_Offsets
title: R5: Offsets, typically in time, at which data values were taken (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `SampledData.offsets` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`SampledData.offsets` 0..1 `string`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `SampledData.offsets` 0..1 `string`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `SampledData.offsets` has a context of SampledData based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `SampledData`.
Element `SampledData.offsets` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Offsets, typically in time, at which data values were taken (new)
element.comment:
Element `SampledData.offsets` has a context of SampledData based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `SampledData`.
Element `SampledData.offsets` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
If offsets is present, the number of data points must be equal to the number of offsets mlutipled by the dimensions.

element.short: Offsets, typically in time, at which data values were taken
element.definition: A series of data points which are decimal values separated by a single space (character u20).  The units in which the offsets are expressed are found in intervalUnit.  The absolute point at which the measurements begin SHALL be conveyed outside the scope of this datatype, e.g. Observation.effectiveDateTime for a timing offset.
element.comment: If offsets is present, the number of data points must be equal to the number of offsets mlutipled by the dimensions.
